title: lasianthus cereiflorus
intro: Lasianthus cereiflorus, also known as the 'Hair Flower' or 'Calyx Flower', is a plant species in the family Theaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Lasianthus cereiflorus is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water, although its use should be limited to small amounts under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
